docTitle: Bible by Map · Bible in One Scroll
metaDesc: Trace the Old Testament timeline, the life of Jesus, and Paul’s journeys on a real map — the Bible happened in real places.

s.title: Bible by Map
s.verse: “For we did not follow cleverly devised myths …” (2 Peter 1:16)
s.purpose: The Bible is not myth. It is the story of real people walking through real places. Trace those paths across a real map, in time order — tap a point, or press ▶ to walk through it step by step.
s.ot_h: From creation to the return to Jerusalem
s.ot_lead: Not a single trip but the flow of redemptive history from creation onward: creation and fall → flood and Babel → a call (Abraham) → the promised land → Exodus → wilderness → conquest → kingdom → division → exile → return.
s.je_kick: ② New Testament — the life of Jesus
s.je_lead: Follow the life of Jesus as the Gospels tell it, in order — from his birth in Bethlehem through his baptism and Galilean ministry, across Samaria, to the cross, resurrection and ascension in Jerusalem.
s.pa_h: From Antioch to the ends of the earth — Rome
s.pa_lead: From Pentecost in Jerusalem, how the gospel spread beyond Judea — through Asia Minor and Europe to the heart of the empire. Pick one of the five journeys (1st·2nd·3rd + the voyage to Rome + after release) and follow the events of Acts with the letters written along the way.
s.foot: Sources · Places follow the biblical text and standard Bible geography (with modern names where helpful). Coastlines, rivers and lakes are drawn from open geographic data (Natural Earth), simplified.

eden.name: Eden (Creation · Fall)
eden.note: Into a world made very good, sin enters and breaks the bond between God and humanity.
events: Creation in six days — “it was very good”
Humanity made in the image of God
The fall, and exile from Eden
The first gospel promise — “the offspring of the woman will crush the serpent’s head”

noah.name: Ararat (the Flood)
noah.note: God judges a world full of sin, yet preserves life through one family.
events: A world filled with corruption
The flood, and Noah’s ark
The rainbow covenant — “never again a flood to destroy”

babel.name: Tower of Babel
babel.note: Against the pride that reaches for heaven, God scatters their speech and the peoples over the earth.
events: “Let us build a city and a tower… and make a name for ourselves”
God confuses their language
The peoples scatter over the earth (Shinar — later Babylon)

ur.name: Ur
ur.note: The Bible’s story of redemption begins with the call of one man.
events: God calls Abram out of Ur of the Chaldeans
“I will make of you a great nation… all peoples blessed through you”
By faith he leaves his homeland

haran.name: Haran
haran.note: On the way, answering the call toward the promised land.
events: Terah’s family settles in Haran
Terah dies
Abram sets out for Canaan at age 75

shechem.name: Shechem · Canaan
shechem.note: In the promised land a family is founded and the covenant passes down the generations.
events: Entering Canaan, first altar — “to your offspring I give this land”
The covenant (Gen 15) · Isaac · Jacob
Jacob’s twelve sons — root of the twelve tribes

egypt.name: Egypt (Goshen)
egypt.note: A family becomes a great nation — then a nation of slaves.
events: Joseph, sold away, rises to rule Egypt
Jacob’s whole family comes down to escape famine
The growing nation of Israel is enslaved

sinai.name: Mt. Sinai
sinai.note: The OT’s central rescue — slaves become God’s own people.
events: The ten plagues and the Passover lamb
The Red Sea parts and they cross
Ten Commandments, the Law, the tabernacle and sacrifices

kadesh.name: Kadesh (wilderness)
kadesh.note: Unbelief costs a generation the land — yet God stays with them.
events: Twelve spies — but the people refuse to trust
38 years in the desert — manna, cloud and fire
On the plains of Moab, Moses’ last sermon (Deuteronomy)

jericho.name: Jericho
jericho.note: At last they enter and take the promised land.
events: The Jordan parts and the people cross
The walls of Jericho fall
Conquest of Canaan and division of the land

shiloh.name: Shiloh (Judges)
shiloh.note: With no king, everyone does as he pleases — a true king is needed.
events: The tabernacle is set up at Shiloh
The cycle of sin–oppression–rescue (Deborah, Gideon, Samson)
Ruth — faithfulness in dark days, in David’s line

jerusalem.name: Jerusalem · United Kingdom
jerusalem.note: Israel’s golden age — and the promise of an everlasting king.
events: Saul the first king, then David and Goliath
The Davidic covenant — “your throne forever” (2 Sam 7)
Solomon builds the temple in Jerusalem

samaria.name: Samaria · Divided Kingdom
samaria.note: The kingdom splits and declines as the prophets cry out.
events: After Solomon, the split into Judah and Israel
Repeated idolatry
Elijah, Isaiah, Jeremiah cry ‘return’ — and foretell the Messiah

nineveh.name: Nineveh (Assyria)
nineveh.note: Ignoring every warning, the north falls first.
events: Nineveh, the capital of Assyria
The northern kingdom falls to Assyria (722 BC)
Jonah is sent to Nineveh

babylon.name: Babylon
babylon.note: The darkest night — yet the promise of restoration remains.
events: Jerusalem falls, the temple is burned (586 BC)
Judah is carried off to Babylon
Daniel and Ezekiel — faith and visions in exile

susa.name: Susa (Persia)
susa.note: Even at the heart of a pagan empire, God keeps his people.
events: Persia overthrows Babylon
Esther saves the Jews from destruction
Nehemiah prays before the king and is sent

return.name: Return to Jerusalem
return.note: They return and rebuild — but no king has come. They await Messiah.
events: Three waves of return by Cyrus’ decree (Zerubbabel 538 · Ezra 458 · Nehemiah 444)
Temple rebuilt and Jerusalem’s walls restored
Malachi — the OT closes: “I will send my messenger”

bethlehem.name: Bethlehem
bethlehem.note: The promised Messiah comes to the town of David — the Word made flesh.
events: Born in Bethlehem, the town of David
Angels announce the news to shepherds
The Magi come to worship

nazareth.name: Nazareth
nazareth.note: God grows up as a man in an ordinary town.
events: Raised in a carpenter’s home
At twelve, asking and answering in the temple
Later rejected in his hometown synagogue

jordan.name: Jordan River
jordan.note: His public ministry begins — heaven seals him as the Son.
events: Baptized by John
The Spirit descends like a dove — “This is my beloved Son”
Overcomes Satan’s three temptations in the wilderness

cana.name: Cana
cana.note: In his first sign he reveals his glory.
events: A guest at a wedding
Water turned to wine — his first sign
His disciples believe in him

sychar.name: Sychar (Samaria)
sychar.note: The early ministry road — through Jerusalem and on to despised Samaria.
events: He cleanses the temple in Jerusalem
By night, Nicodemus — “You must be born again”
Living water for a Samaritan woman

capernaum.name: Capernaum
capernaum.note: Home base of the Galilean ministry — calling, teaching and opposition all meet here.
events: Calls the fishermen and bases himself here
The Sermon on the Mount — “Blessed are the poor in spirit”
Parables of the kingdom — and rising opposition

galilee.name: Sea of Galilee
galilee.note: With power over nature he shows who he is.
events: “Peace, be still” — he calms the storm
Feeds 5,000 with five loaves and two fish
Walks on the water

caesarea_p.name: Caesarea Philippi
caesarea_p.note: The disciples grasp that he is the Christ, and the way of the cross opens.
events: Peter’s confession — “You are the Christ, the Son of the living God”
He first foretells his death and resurrection
Transfigured in glory on the mountain

jericho.name: Jericho
jericho.note: On the road up to Jerusalem, he seeks the lost.
events: He gives sight to blind Bartimaeus
He calls Zacchaeus the tax collector
“The Son of Man came to seek and save the lost”

bethany.name: Bethany
bethany.note: He shows mastery over death on the eve of his final week.
events: He raises Lazarus from the tomb — “I am the resurrection and the life”
Mary anoints him for burial
This sign hardens the plot to kill him

jerusalem.name: Jerusalem
jerusalem.note: The climax the whole Bible points to — cross, resurrection, ascension.
events: Triumphal entry; he overturns the temple tables again
At the Last Supper he washes the disciples’ feet
He dies on the cross
Risen on the third day, then ascended

antioch.name: Antioch
antioch.note: The sending church where the Gentile mission begins and returns.
events: In prayer the Spirit sets apart Barnabas and Saul
The church where believers were first called ‘Christians’
Launch point of the 1st, 2nd and 3rd journeys

seleucia.name: Seleucia
seleucia.note: Antioch’s port — they set sail for Cyprus.

salamis.name: Salamis (Cyprus)
salamis.note: First stop on Cyprus — preaching in the synagogues.

paphos.name: Paphos
paphos.note: Power is shown against those who oppose the gospel.
events: Elymas the magician is struck blind
Sergius Paulus the proconsul believes
From here ‘Paul’ takes the lead

perga.name: Perga
perga.note: A port in Pamphylia — here John Mark turns back.

pantioch.name: Pisidian Antioch
pantioch.note: The gospel breaks open toward the Gentiles.
events: A long sermon of salvation history in the synagogue
Many Gentiles rejoice and receive the word
Driven out by opposition

iconium.name: Iconium
iconium.note: Many believe, but persecution forces them on.

lystra.name: Lystra
lystra.note: Hailed as a god, then stoned.
events: Heals a man lame from birth
The crowd tries to sacrifice to them as gods
Stoned and left for dead, he gets up

derbe.name: Derbe
derbe.note: Many disciples are made; they retrace their steps to strengthen the churches.

troas.name: Troas
troas.note: The turning point where the gospel first heads for Europe.
events: A vision of a man of Macedonia
“Come over and help us”
They cross at once into Europe

philippi.name: Philippi
philippi.note: Europe’s first church — the gospel triumphs even in prison.
events: Lydia, a dealer in purple, believes
A fortune-telling slave girl is freed
Midnight hymns, an earthquake, the jailer’s household saved

thess.name: Thessalonica
thess.note: The word shakes the city and opposition boils over.
events: Reasons in the synagogue three Sabbaths
“These who have turned the world upside down”
They slip away by night

berea.name: Berea
berea.note: The ‘noble’ ones who examined the word for themselves.
events: They receive the word eagerly
Daily they examine the Scriptures to check it
Many come to believe

athens.name: Athens
athens.note: In the city of philosophers he proclaims the ‘unknown god’.
events: Provoked by a city full of idols
On the Areopagus he preaches Creator and resurrection
Mocked, yet a few believe

corinth.name: Corinth
corinth.note: He stays a year and a half, planting a church in a city of vice.
events: Makes tents with Aquila and Priscilla
“Do not be afraid… for I am with you”
Many Corinthians believe and are baptized

ephesus.name: Ephesus
ephesus.note: Heart of the third journey — the word spreads across Asia.
events: Daily teaching for two years in the hall of Tyrannus
Magic books burned; powerful works
The silversmith Demetrius starts a riot

miletus.name: Miletus
miletus.note: A tearful farewell to the Ephesian elders.
events: “Care for the church he bought with his own blood”
The elders weep, knowing they’ll not see him again

caesarea.name: Caesarea
caesarea.note: For two years he defends the gospel before governors and a king.
events: Trials before governors Felix and Festus
His defense before King Agrippa
“I appeal to Caesar” — on to Rome

jerusalem.name: Jerusalem
jerusalem.note: Seized in the temple, his road as a prisoner begins.
events: Seized by the crowd in the temple
Defends himself before the council
As a Roman citizen, sent under guard to Caesarea

myra.name: Myra
myra.note: They transfer to an Alexandrian ship bound for Rome.

fairhaven.name: Fair Havens (Crete)
fairhaven.note: Paul’s warning to stop sailing goes unheeded.

crete.name: Crete
crete.note: After his release, the island where he leaves Titus to set the churches in order.
events: Leaves Titus to appoint elders in every town
Calls for blameless, faithful leaders
Writes the letter to Titus

malta.name: Malta
malta.note: Through storm and shipwreck, God’s promise holds.
events: The ‘northeaster’ wrecks the ship, yet all 276 survive
Bitten by a viper, he is unharmed
He heals the sick of the island

syracuse.name: Syracuse
syracuse.note: They stay three days in Sicily.

puteoli.name: Puteoli
puteoli.note: They land in Italy and are met by fellow believers.

rome.name: Rome
rome.note: The gospel reaches the heart of the empire at last — not an end but a beginning.
events: Two years under house arrest, boldly proclaiming the kingdom
From here he writes the Prison Letters (Eph·Phil·Col·Phm)
Later imprisoned again, writes 2 Timothy and is martyred — “I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race”
